info.name: EPUB Translator API
info.description:
Endpoints for submitting and tracking EPUB translation jobs, social login (Google/GitHub OAuth), and Stripe billing.

Auth is opt-in via AUTH_ENABLED: when false (default), every endpoint below works without a token and jobs are unowned. When true, /translations* endpoints accept an optional Bearer token (enable the disabled Authorization header on each request) and jobs become scoped to the caller.

Payment is opt-in via PAYMENT_ENABLED (requires AUTH_ENABLED=true): when true, POST /translations returns a Stripe checkout_url instead of starting the job immediately; the job only starts once the /billing/webhook receives checkout.session.completed.

variable.description: JWT returned by /auth/google/callback or /auth/github/callback. Only relevant when AUTH_ENABLED=true.

request.description: Redirects (302) to Google's OAuth consent screen with a signed CSRF state param. Open this URL directly in a browser rather than 'Send' in Postman — the flow needs an actual Google sign-in UI and ends by redirecting back to GOOGLE_REDIRECT_URI (google/callback) with ?code=&state=.

query.description: Authorization code Google appends to the redirect URI after consent
Signed state Google echoes back verbatim (CSRF check, TTL = OAUTH_STATE_TTL_SECONDS)

request.description: Called by Google's redirect after the user consents (not meant to be invoked manually with fake values — code/state must come from a live Google login flow). Exchanges the code, upserts the user, and returns {access_token, token_type}.

Redirects (302) to GitHub's OAuth consent screen with a signed CSRF state param. Open this URL directly in a browser rather than 'Send' in Postman — the flow needs an actual GitHub sign-in UI and ends by redirecting back to GITHUB_REDIRECT_URI (github/callback) with ?code=&state=.

query.description: Authorization code GitHub appends to the redirect URI after consent
Signed state GitHub echoes back verbatim (CSRF check, TTL = OAUTH_STATE_TTL_SECONDS)

request.description: Called by GitHub's redirect after the user consents (not meant to be invoked manually with fake values — code/state must come from a live GitHub login flow). Exchanges the code, upserts the user, and returns {access_token, token_type}.

header.description: Enable and set access_token when AUTH_ENABLED=true

request.description: Returns {user: {id, provider, email, name}} for the token's owner, or {user: null} if unauthenticated or AUTH_ENABLED=false.

header.description: Enable and set access_token when AUTH_ENABLED=true, to own this job

formdata.description: One of REPLACE, APPEND_TEXT, APPEND_BLOCK. Defaults to APPEND_BLOCK if omitted.

request.description:
Uploads an .epub file and starts a translation job (or, if PAYMENT_ENABLED=true, creates a Stripe Checkout Session instead of starting it). Response (202) is either:
- Payment disabled: {job_id, status} — status is 'queued', job runs immediately.
- Payment enabled: {job_id, status: 'awaiting_payment', checkout_url} — the job only starts after Stripe confirms payment via POST /billing/webhook. Open checkout_url in a browser to pay.

header.description: Enable and set access_token when AUTH_ENABLED=true, to list only your own jobs

request.description: Returns a list of jobs (all of them if unauthenticated/AUTH_ENABLED=false, otherwise only jobs owned by the caller), in the order they were created. Each item is the same shape as GET /translations/{job_id}.

header.description: Enable and set access_token when AUTH_ENABLED=true, if the job has an owner

request.description: Returns: job_id, source_filename, target_language, submit_kind, concurrency, created_at, status (queued|awaiting_payment|running|cancelling|completed|failed|cancelled), progress, error, warning, owner_user_id, user_prompt, estimated_tokens, price_cents, currency, stripe_payment_status. Returns 404 (not 403) if the job belongs to a different user, so job existence isn't leaked.

header.description: Enable and set access_token when AUTH_ENABLED=true, if the job has an owner

request.description: Requests cancellation of a queued or running job and discards any partial output. Returns the job with status 'cancelled' (if it hadn't started yet) or 'cancelling' (if it was already running, until the background worker notices and finalizes it as 'cancelled'). Returns 409 if the job already completed, failed, or was cancelled; 404 if it belongs to another user.

header.description: Enable and set access_token when AUTH_ENABLED=true, if the job has an owner

request.description: Server-Sent Events stream with job status updates (same shape as GET /translations/{job_id}), plus a ': keep-alive' comment every 15s, until the job reaches a terminal status (completed/failed/cancelled). Use Postman's 'Send' (not code generation) to watch the stream in the response pane.

header.description: Enable and set access_token when AUTH_ENABLED=true, if the job has an owner

request.description: Downloads the translated .epub once the job status is 'completed'. Returns 409 if not completed yet, 404 if the job belongs to another user.
Static HTML confirmation page. This is the Stripe Checkout success_url — Stripe redirects the payer's browser here after payment; not meant to be called directly except to preview the page.
Static HTML cancellation page. This is the Stripe Checkout cancel_url — Stripe redirects the payer's browser here if they abandon checkout; not meant to be called directly except to preview the page.

header.description: Required. HMAC signature over the raw body, verified against STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET — Postman can't forge this. Use the Stripe CLI instead: `stripe listen --forward-to {{base_url}}/billing/webhook` then `stripe trigger checkout.session.completed`.

request.description: Stripe webhook endpoint. Reads the raw request body and verifies Stripe-Signature against STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET — a request without a valid signature (like one sent straight from Postman) returns 400. On checkout.session.completed, marks the job paid and submits it for translation. Only handles this one event type; any other event, or one whose job_id doesn't resolve or isn't awaiting_payment, returns 200 {status: 'ignored'} (never 500 — Stripe retries indefinitely on failure). Test via the Stripe CLI, not Postman.
